title: RTL + positional-form rendering support (Arabic/Hebrew-ready)
context: Arabic and Hebrew are underserved and frequently mangled by generic tools — wrong directionality, wrong contextual forms (Arabic isolated/initial/medial/final), wrong stroke order. The renderer must handle RTL layout and select the correct positional form per the model before any RTL script can ship. This work is medium-risk because incorrect RTL or joining behavior in a practice sheet directly teaches the wrong formation. A script reviewer must confirm direction and joining on a sample before merge.
objective: Extend the SVG renderer to correctly lay out RTL scripts (right-to-left text flow and guide progression) and select the correct positional form (isolated/initial/medial/final) from the GlyphModel's positionalForms field, with golden-file tests and script reviewer confirmation.
acceptanceCriteria: Renderer correctly lays out RTL scripts: text/glyph flow and guideline progression go right-to-left when ScriptModel.direction == 'rtl'.
Renderer selects the correct positional form (isolated/initial/medial/final) from GlyphModel.positionalForms based on the glyph's position in a joining sequence.
Golden-file SVG tests cover: an RTL sample sheet (e.g. isolated Arabic/Hebrew letters) and a joining sequence sample demonstrating form selection; both are byte-stable across runs.
Script reviewer (script-literate for an RTL script) confirms direction and joining behavior on the sample sheet.
Determinism preserved; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass; DCO signed-off.
resources: PLAN.md section 6 (GlyphModel.positionalForms, ScriptModel.direction, RTL handling), section 9 M2
schema-001 (positionalForms and direction fields in the schema)
output: RTL layout and positional-form selection implemented in packages/core, with golden-file SVG tests for RTL and joining samples — committed via PR with DCO sign-off.
